content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

March 4, 1996

Mr. Daniel Hentschke
City Attorney
City of Oceanside
Office of the City Attorney
300 North Coast Highway
Oceanside, California 92054

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-047

Dear Mr. Hentschke:

This is in response to your question regarding the
Commission's policy for providing advice under the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Pursuant to Section 83114(b) and Commission Regulation 18329,
the Commission provides formal written advice and informal
assistance to public officials concerning their duties under the
Act. As we discussed, however, it has long been the Commission's
policy not to provide written advice or telephonic assistance to a
party involved in litigation, if the advice request contains an
issue that the court may consider. (Alperin Advice Letter,
No. I-86-111; and Woodside Advice Letter, No. A-85-106.)

The Commission believes that once a matter concerning a
public official's duties under the Act is pending before a court,
the determination of the law's application is in the hands of the
judicial branch.

_________________________________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18995.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at 916/322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[illegible signature]

By: Hyla P. Wagner
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: What is the FPPC's policy for providing written advice and assistance to public officials under the Political Reform Act, particularly regarding matters pending in litigation?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC does not provide written advice or telephonic assistance to parties involved in litigation when the advice request contains issues that a court may consider, as the determination of the law's application belongs to the judicial branch once the matter is pending before a court.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; extracted question and conclusion from body text. Document is a policy clarification letter rather than analysis of a specific factual scenario. Dates and citations are clear and well-documented.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the FPPC's policy for providing written advice and assistance to public officials under the Political Reform Act, particularly regarding matters pending in litigation?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC does not provide written advice or telephonic assistance to parties involved in litigation when the advice request contains issues that a court may consider, as the determination of the law's application belongs to the judicial branch once the matter is pending before a court.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the City Attorney of Oceanside regarding the Commission's policy on providing advice to public officials under the Political Reform Act, clarifying that the FPPC does not provide formal written advice or informal assistance in matters pending before courts.
